channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy buys 3,273 bitcoin as it inches closer to its 1 million target
item.description: Strategy, the largest corporate holder of bitcoin in the world, now holds 818,334 BTC, which it said it purchased for nearly $62 billion at an average price of roughly $75,537 per token....
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.title: Deepfake Voice Attacks are Outpacing Defenses: What Security Leaders Should Know

item.title: Trigona Affiliates Deploy Custom Exfiltration Tool to Streamline Data Theft
item.description: From the so-there-they-are! dept Out of sight, out of mind? It seems like ages ago that DataBreaches last reported on the Trigona ransomware group, but it was actually in September 2023. After that, D...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Bad Memories Still Haunt AI Agents
item.description: Cisco found and fixed a significant vulnerability in the way Anthropic handles memories, but experts warn that mishandled memory files will continue to threaten AI systems....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: Bitwarden CLI, the command-line interface for the password manager Bitwarden, has reportedly been compromised as part of a newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to fi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Half a million Britons’ medical data were offered for sale on Alibaba in major UK Biobank breach
item.description: Harry Taylor reports: Data from 500,000 people who volunteered their health information to the UK Biobank has been breached and offered for sale online in China. Technology Minister Ian Murray said th...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: RAMP Uncovered: Anatomy of Russia’s Ransomware Marketplace
item.description: Pierluigi Paganini reports: A leaked database from RAMP gives us a rare look behind the curtain. It shows how cybercrime works when it becomes structured, commercial, and repeatable. Instead of random...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Mile Bluff Medical Center says security incident that involved data encryption disrupted phone, computer systems
item.description: DysruptionHub reports: Mile Bluff Medical Center in Mauston, Wisconsin, said Tuesday that a security event involving data encryption disrupted some phone and computer functions, prompting clinical tea...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Andre Cronje’s Flying Tulip adds withdrawal circuit breaker as DeFi exploits mount
item.description: Flying Tulip said its withdrawal safeguard is designed to fail open, while a status page lets users monitor the system in real time....
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph - Security

item.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
item.description: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated, larg...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Project Glasswing Proved AI Can Find the Bugs. Who's Going to Fix Them?
item.description: Last week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, an AI model so effective at discovering software vulnerabilities that they took the extraordinary step of postponing its public release. Instead, the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
item.description: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
item.description: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide arra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
